Taizhou Wanli Lighting Co., Ltd. presents a clear case of industry pivoting and geographic clustering. Originally established as a trading company in Shijiazhuang ('Shijiazhuang Yuanheng Lisheng Commerce and Trade Co.'), the entity has relocated to Linhai City in Taizhou, Zhejiang. Crucially, it has embedded itself within the 'Linhai Lantern Entrepreneurship Park'. This move aligns perfectly with Linhai's status as a major manufacturing and export hub for festive and decorative lighting in China. The company's micro-scale (0 insured employees, 100k RMB paid-in capital) and location in an 'entrepreneurship park' suggest it operates as a small-scale assembly workshop or an e-commerce trading node leveraging the local lighting supply chain.
